channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Hall of ‘Eternal Flame’ Burns Down in Japan
item.description: The sacred site of a flame that was lit more than 1,200 years ago, according to Buddhist spiritual leaders, burned down on Wednesday in southwestern Japan.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Makoto Kondo/The Yomiuri Shimbun, via Reuters

item.title: You Can Give Knicks Fans T-Shirts. But Will They Wear Them? Well …
item.description: In arenas all across the league, N.B.A. fans embrace the monochrome T-shirt as a way to show team spirit. New Yorkers aren’t having it.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Keh

media:content.media:credit: Pamela Smith/Getty Images

item.title: El gobierno de Trump y sus medidas para beneficiar sus finanzas personales
item.description: Ningún presidente ha utilizado el gobierno federal para promover sus intereses personales y los de su familia y aliados de forma tan expansiva y abierta como Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Donald Trump parece aún menos inhibido por las normas, escritas o no, que rigieron a sus predecesores.

item.title: Nunca ha habido un ejemplo de corrupción presidencial como este
item.description: Entre exenciones fiscales, acuerdos judiciales favorables y la creación de un fondo de 1800 millones de dólares, el presidente Trump ha profundizado el uso de las instituciones federales para protegerse.
item.dc:creator: El Comité Editorial

media:content.media:credit: Ilustración fotográfica de Philotheus Nisch para The New York Times

item.title: Footprints Around a Phone
item.description: What do you think this image is communicating?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Lights, Camera, Budget: Mamdani’s Municipal Spectacle
item.description: Mayor Mamdani’s executive budget was met with hero worship online. What did the social media chatter miss about the messy reality of municipal policymaking?
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s executive budget release earlier this month was given the star treatment by some of his followers on social media.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for May 21, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: He Sued the N.Y.P.D. He Advised ‘Homeland.’ Now He’s Mamdani’s Lawyer.
item.description: Ramzi Kassem is accustomed to challenging authority. As the chief counsel to Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York City, he finds himself on the other side.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ramzi Kassem, who once sued the New York Police Department for its surveillance of Muslim Americans, is now advising the mayor on police matters.

item.title: C.E.O. Behind Huge ‘Vote for Trump’ Sign Has a New Slogan: Vote for Him
item.description: Anthony Constantino has been endorsed by President Trump in his bid to succeed Representative Elise Stefanik in New York. But could his provocative campaign cost the G.O.P. a seat?
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Adrianna Newell for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Anthony Constantino erected a huge ‘Vote for Trump’ sign in 2024 atop his company’s headquarters in Amsterdam, N.Y., and has since won the president’s endorsement.

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘imbroglio’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 20 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ¿Sudar más mejora el entrenamiento?
item.description: Entender cuánto sudas puede ayudarte a mantenerte hidratado y seguro mientras entrenas en condiciones más cálidas. Hemos preguntado a los expertos a qué hay que prestar atención.
item.dc:creator: Allessandra DiCorato

media:content.media:credit: Eric Helgas for The New York Times

item.title: Word of the Day: imbroglio
item.description: This word has appeared in 20 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: 3 Die After Suspected Exposure to Unknown Substance in New Mexico
item.description: More than a dozen emergency officials were hospitalized after responding to a home in a town near Albuquerque. The authorities said there was no threat to the public.
item.dc:creator: Yan Zhuang

media:content.media:credit: Savannah Peters/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Three people have died after being exposed to an unidentified substance in Mountainair, N.M.

item.title: Jimmy Kimmel Pays His Respects to ‘The Late Show with Stephen Colbert’
item.description: Kimmel said he hoped others will also tune in to CBS “for the last time” to watch Thursday’s “Late Show” finale.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Hollywood Ghosted the Cannes Film Festival This Year
item.description: Fewer movie stars, fewer oligarch megayachts in the bay, fewer parties and ever more desperate paparazzi.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Waxman

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Sam Whitney/The New York Times; source photographs by adventtr/Getty Images

item.title: Gisèle Pelicot desafía la narrativa de las víctimas
item.description: Las memorias de Pelicot cuestionan las narrativas convencionales sobre las víctimas y muestran cómo funciona la negación incluso después del horror.
item.dc:creator: Parul Sehgal
item.category: A Hymn to Life: Shame Has to Change Sides (Book)

item.title: El ICE detiene a un migrante hondureño en un tribunal pese a una orden del juez
item.description: Una persona hondureña fue detenida el martes en Nueva York, lo que, según dijeron sus abogados, violaba una prohibición impuesta un día antes. Horas después, el hombre fue puesto en libertad.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich and Benjamin Weiser

media:content.media:credit: Madison Swart para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Los agentes federales enmascarados se habían convertido en una presencia habitual en los pasillos del 26 de Federal Plaza, en el Bajo Manhattan.

item.title: A Sexy Garden Ruffles the Chelsea Flower Show
item.description: At the marquee event of the U.K. gardening calendar, one designer wants horticultural enthusiasts to consider the birds and the bees.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands

media:content.media:credit: Aaron Chown/Press Association, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: “Aphrodite’s Hothouse” explores carnal themes through the medium of houseplants and adult toys.

item.title: The Hard-Line Military Fraternity Running Iran
item.description: Decision making in Iran is guided by a small group of men associated with the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A billboard in Tehran showing the three supreme leaders, Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomenei, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and his son, Mojtaba Khamenei.

item.title: Mira Nair Finds a New Audience as Mother of Zohran Mamdani
item.description: “I feel like we have given him to the world,” the filmmaker said in a conversation with The New York Times. Her next movie is about one of India’s greatest artists.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das

media:content.media:credit: Atul Loke for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mira Nair, the acclaimed film director and mother of Zohran Mamdani, in Amritsar, India, in March.

item.title: Corrections: May 21, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, May 21, 2026.

item.title: China’s Housing Slump Shows Signs of Bottoming Out. We’ve Been Here Before.
item.description: Property prices in Shanghai, in particular, are rebounding, but the national market still faces an enormous overhang — 90 million empty or unfinished apartments.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Many families in China poured their savings into apartments once seen as a safe path to wealth, only to watch values unravel.

item.title: How China’s Housing Crisis Has Global Consequences
item.description: Our Beijing bureau chief, Keith Bradsher, describes how China’s enormous housing crash is affecting economies all over the world.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher, Rebecca Suner, Thomas Vollkommer, Joey Sendaydiego and Luke Piotrowski

item.title: Quote of the Day: Islamophobia Spreads Fast, As Does Fear
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, May 21, 2026.

item.title: Despite Problems, Boeing Sought Less Inspection of Part Involved in UPS Crash
item.description: The National Transportation Board has been investigating the cause behind a plane crash in Louisville, Ky., that killed 15 people last year.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz

media:content.media:credit: NTSB, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: A drone view of the crash site next to a runway at the Muhammad Ali International Airport after the crash of a UPS cargo plane in Louisville, Ky., last year.

item.title: Colorado Governor Censured for Commuting Sentence of Election Denier
item.description: The Colorado State Democratic Party, reflecting the anger of rank-and-file Democratic voters, rebuked Gov. Jared Polis on Wednesday over his decision to release Tina Peters from prison.
item.dc:creator: Jack Healy

media:content.media:credit: Michael Ciaglo for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “The man is wildly out of touch with the party he effectively leads.” Gov. Jared Polis of Colorado, a Democrat, infuriated the party faithful when he commuted the prison sentence of an election denier.

item.title: A $5 Million Donation From Big Tobacco Preceded F.D.A. Vape Decision
item.description: Reynolds American’s contribution to MAGA Inc. came about one week before the Trump administration announced a new policy on e-cigarettes the company had sought.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth P. Vogel and Christina Jewett

media:content.media:credit: Stephanie Keith/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The F.D.A. has issued new guidance that could pave the way for major tobacco companies including Reynolds American to begin selling flavored vapes.

item.title: Un portaaviones de EE. UU. arriba al Caribe mientras Trump presiona a Cuba
item.description: El Nimitz y sus buques de guerra escolta entraron en el mar Caribe el mismo día en que el Departamento de Justicia de Estados Unidos anunció su imputación a Raúl Castro.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Pablo Porciuncula/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: El portaaviones USS Nimitz en la bahía de Guanabara, en Río de Janeiro, a principios de este mes.

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for May 21, 2026
item.description: Zhou Zhang and Mallory Montgomery have fashioned a superb Thursday puzzle.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Joshua Bright for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: This Thursday puzzle should go down smoother than a hazy I.P.A. (I’m more of a lager guy.)

item.title: The A.I. threat to audiobooks
item.description: Artificial intelligence has made pirated audiobooks faster to make and harder to detect. Our reporter Alexandra Alter tells us about the latest threat to the publishing industry.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Alter, Léo Hamelin and Laura Salaberry
item.category: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone (Book)

item.title: G.O.P. May Bear The Cost of Trump’s Unpopularity
item.description: Donald Trump’s endorsed candidates are winning Republican primaries across the country, but the president’s unpopularity with the broader electorate could drag the party down in the general election, the Times political correspondent Shane Goldmacher explains.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher, Nour Idriss, Stephanie Swart and Rafaela Balster

item.title: Nimitz Aircraft Carrier Enters Caribbean as Trump Pressures Cuba
item.description: The carrier arrived in the southern Caribbean on the same day that the Justice Department announced charges against Raul Castro.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Pablo Porciuncula/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The aircraft carrier U.S.S. Nimitz in Guanabara Bay in Rio de Janeiro, earlier this month.

item.title: News of U.S. Indictment Slow to Reach Cubans Waiting for a Breakthrough
item.description: While many Cubans were divided over the legitimacy of the U.S. charging Raul Castro with murder, the hope for developments that might ease their suffering is widespread. “This has to change.”
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola

media:content.media:credit: Lisette Poole González for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A furniture workshop in Havana carries reminders that the Castro family has been part of Cuba’s ruling elite for more than 65 years.

item.title: Trump’s Praise of Spencer Pratt Could Doom Him in L.A. Mayor’s Race
item.description: Democrats have been trying to portray Mr. Pratt, a former reality TV star, as a MAGA Republican in the mayor’s race. President Trump just gave them a helping hand.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla
item.category: Los Angeles County Federation of Labor

media:content.media:credit: Maggie Shannon for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Spencer Pratt stands in the ruins of his home, destroyed in the Palisades fire in early 2025. He is now a Los Angeles mayoral contender.

item.title: Meta despide a 8000 empleados, a medida que aumenta el uso de la IA
item.description: La agitación en Meta —propietaria de Facebook, WhatsApp e Instagram— ofrece una visión cercana de los despidos en la era de la inteligencia artificial.
item.dc:creator: Eli Tan, Kalley Huang and Mike Isaac

media:content.media:credit: Ian C. Bates para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Sede de Meta en Menlo Park, California. La empresa, que cambió su nombre para el metaverso, se está transformando para la era de la inteligencia artificial.

item.title: Investigators Reveal How a Faulty Part Led to the UPS Plane Crash
item.description: On Tuesday, investigators suggested that infrequent maintenance requirements and other issues appeared to lead to the deadly UPS cargo plane crash in Louisville, Ky.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Jon Cherry/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Plumes of smoke near the area of a UPS cargo plane crash at Louisville Muhammad Ali International Airport in November.

item.title: Trump’s Government Moves to Spare an Unhappy Taxpayer Named Trump
item.description: No president has ever used the federal government to advance his own personal interests and those of his family and allies as expansively and openly as Mr. Trump has.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump seems even less inhibited by the rules, written or unwritten, that governed his predecessors.

item.title: Pigeons and People Have Been Frenemies for Longer Than You Think
item.description: Bones discovered at an archaeological site in Cyprus suggest the birds have been strutting around human settlements since at least 1400 B.C.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Nuwer

media:content.media:credit: Angelina Katsanis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A new study leads scientists to wonder whether people set out to domesticate pigeons or the process occurred naturally as pigeons “colonized” human environments.

item.title: Judge Rules That White House Officials Must Follow Presidential Records Act
item.description: The judge overruled a government memo from April, which rejected a records law that mandates preservation of all official presidential documents.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The White House had issued guidance in April suggesting that officials did not need to preserve some of their text messages.

item.title: Trump’s Coast Guard Address: ‘Good Looking Men’ and Deja Vu
item.description: President Trump’s commencement address was a mixture of jokes and praise for the seafaring life of the Coast Guard graduates. But there were detours into choppy waters.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Donald J. Trump at the U.S. Coast Guard Academy in New London, Conn., on Wednesday. It was the second time he delivered the commencement address as commander in chief.

item.title: Senate G.O.P. Ready to Drop Ballroom Funds From ICE Bill
item.description: Several Republicans had balked at including the money in an immigration enforcement measure that the party plans to push through on a party-line vote.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.category: White House East Wing and Ballroom (Washington, DC)

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump gave reporters a tour Tuesday of the White House ballroom construction project.

item.title: Was a Story That Just Won a Literary Prize A.I.-Generated?
item.description: A respected literary magazine has published an award-winning short story many readers believe to be generated by artificial intelligence. Experts aren’t all so sure.
item.dc:creator: Valeriya Safronova

media:content.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times

item.title: How the $1.8 Billion Trump Fund May Violate Past Practice and Policy
item.description: The fund that could offer payouts to Trump allies who accuse the government of mistreatment is not only highly unusual but also appears to violate the administration’s own policies.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: On Pam Bondi’s first day as attorney general in February 2025, she signed a directive that appeared to prohibit the kind of fund the Trump administration established this week.

item.title: Cuba dice que está dispuesta a negociar
item.description: El embajador de Cuba ante las Naciones Unidas expresó en una entrevista que La Habana quería dialogar, pero que el gobierno de Trump estaba creando pretextos para emprender una acción militar.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas

media:content.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ernesto Soberón Guzmán, embajador de Cuba ante la ONU, el miércoles en Nueva York.

item.title: Soundtrack to 8,000 Job Cuts: A Meta Worker’s Layoff-Themed A.I. Songs
item.description: On a dark day of layoffs at Meta, one employee responded by creating an internal radio station that plays songs about job cuts — generated by artificial intelligence, of course.
item.dc:creator: Mike Isaac

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times

item.title: Thomas Massie, a Trump Critic, Causes Latest Midterm Fight Over Israel
item.description: In primary races across the country, debates over Israel are taking center stage, dividing Democrats and Republicans alike.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Lerer and Jennifer Medina

media:content.media:credit: Jon Cherry/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: “I would have come out sooner, but I had to call my opponent and concede, and it took a while to find Ed Gallrein in Tel Aviv,” Representative Thomas Massie said in his concession speech on Tuesday night, referring to his Republican opponent.

item.title: The U.S. Charges Raúl Castro, Increasing the Pressure on Cuba
item.description: Also, Jan. 6 police officers sue to block Trump’s new fund. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Ismael Francisco/Associated Press

item.title: Thailand Tightens Visa Rules for Tourists From Dozens of Nations, Including U.S.
item.description: The country had loosened entry requirements after the pandemic to attract visitors, but will now limit many travelers to 30 days without a visa.
item.dc:creator: Gabe Castro-Root

media:content.media:credit: Lauren DeCicca for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The new visa policy affects citizens of the United States, Canada, France, Germany, Japan, Singapore and other countries.

item.title: Video Shows Israeli Minister Taunting Detained Pro-Palestinian Activists
item.description: Itamar Ben-Gvir, the Israeli national security minister, posted a one-minute video showing him taunting pro-Palestinian activists who were handcuffed and pinned down to the deck of a ship. The video drew condemnation both in Israel and abroad.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper

media:content.media:credit: Amir Levy/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Israel’s national security minister, Itamar Ben Gvir, in the Israeli parliament, on Wednesday.

item.title: First North Korean Athletes Play in South Korea in Nearly 8 Years
item.description: Volunteers cheered for both North and South Korean teams as they faced off in an Asian Football Confederation Women’s Champions League semifinal on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Meg Felling and Chang W. Lee

media:content.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Chung Kyung-jo, center, waving, supported the visiting team in the match between two Korean teams, in Suwon, South Korea on Wednesday.
